body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-31237", "cvss": {"score": 9.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-502", "name": "Deserialization of Untrusted Data"}], "description": "The Ludwig framework thru 0.10.4 is vulnerable to insecure deserialization (CWE-502) through its predict() method. When a user provides a dataset file path to the predict() method, the framework automatically determines the file format. If the file is a pickle (.pkl) file, it is loaded using pandas.read_pickle() without any validation or security restrictions. This allows the deserialization of arbitrary Python objects via the unsafe pickle module. A remote attacker can exploit this by providing a maliciously crafted pickle file, leading to arbitrary code execution on the system running the Ludwig prediction.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.006, "percentile": 0.44505}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-wcr3-gm9f-f87q", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-27T22:19:35Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wcr3-gm9f-f87q", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-wcr3-gm9f-f87q"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-31237"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-12T18:16:52Z", "published_at": "2026-05-12T18:30:41Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-31237", "https://github.com/ludwig-ai/ludwig", "https://www.notion.so/CVE-2026-31237-35d1e139318881fb95a2ee7c5d0e17d8", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wcr3-gm9f-f87q"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/ludwig-ai/ludwig", "summary": "Ludwig framework is vulnerable to insecure deserialization through its predict() method.", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-27T22:19:35Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-wcr3-gm9f-f87q", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "ludwig"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.10.4"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Ludwig framework is vulnerable to insecure deserialization through its predict() method.
